scope_summary: WA's principal environmental statute. It establishes the Environmental Protection Authority, provides for environmental protection policies, environmental impact assessment of proposals and of planning schemes (Part IV, including assessment of region and local planning schemes, State planning policies and planning codes referred under the Planning and Development Act 2005), and regulates pollution, environmental harm, clearing of native vegetation (clearing permits, environmentally sensitive areas), prescribed premises, works approvals and licences, and enforcement. Applies state-wide in Western Australia.

cross_references.cited: Waste Avoidance and Resource Recovery Act 2007
